greek: ὅταν δὲ ἔλθῃ τὸ τέλειον, τὸ ἐκ μέρους καταργηθήσεται.
kjv: But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.
modern: But when the complete comes, what is partial will be brought to an end.

greek: Ἡ ἀγάπη μακροθυμεῖ, χρηστεύεται ἡ ἀγάπη, οὐ ζηλοῖ ἡ ἀγάπη, οὐ περπερεύεται, οὐ φυσιοῦται,
kjv: Charity suffereth long, and is kind; charity envieth not; charity vaunteth not itself, is not puffed up,
modern: Love is patient; love is kind; love does not envy; it does not boast; it is not puffed up.

greek: οὐκ ἀσχημονεῖ, οὐ ζητεῖ τὰ ἑαυτῆς, οὐ παροξύνεται, οὐ λογίζεται τὸ κακόν,
kjv: Doth not behave itself unseemly, seeketh not her own, is not easily provoked, thinketh no evil;
modern: It does not behave disgracefully, does not seek its own interests, is not provoked, does not keep account of wrong;

greek: καὶ ἐὰν ψωμίσω πάντα τὰ ὑπάρχοντά μου, καὶ ἐὰν παραδῶ τὸ σῶμά μου, ἵνα καυθήσομαι, ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω, οὐδὲν ὠφελοῦμαι.
kjv: And though I bestow all my goods to feed the poor, and though I give my body to be burned, and have not charity, it profiteth me nothing.
modern: And if I parcel out all my possessions, and if I hand over my body so that I will be burned, but do not have love, I gain nothing.

greek: Ἡ ἀγάπη οὐδέποτε πίπτει. εἴτε δὲ προφητεῖαι, καταργηθήσονται· εἴτε γλῶσσαι, παύσονται· εἴτε γνῶσις, καταργηθήσεται.
kjv: Charity never faileth: but whether there be prophecies, they shall fail; whether there be tongues, they shall cease; whether there be knowledge, it shall vanish away.
modern: Love never falls. But whether prophecies, they will be brought to an end; whether tongues, they will cease; whether knowledge, it will be brought to an end.

greek: οὐ χαίρει ἐπὶ τῇ ἀδικίᾳ, συγχαίρει δὲ τῇ ἀληθείᾳ·
kjv: Rejoiceth not in iniquity, but rejoiceth in the truth;
modern: It does not rejoice over injustice, but rejoices with the truth.

greek: Ἐὰν ταῖς γλώσσαις τῶν ἀνθρώπων λαλῶ καὶ τῶν ἀγγέλων, ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω, γέγονα χαλκὸς ἠχῶν ἢ κύμβαλον ἀλαλάζον.
kjv: Though I speak with the tongues of men and of angels, and have not charity, I am become as sounding brass, or a tinkling cymbal.
modern: If I speak in the tongues of humans and of angels but do not have love, I have become sounding bronze or a clanging cymbal.

greek: καὶ ἐὰν ἔχω προφητείαν καὶ εἰδῶ τὰ μυστήρια πάντα καὶ πᾶσαν τὴν γνῶσιν, καὶ ἐὰν ἔχω πᾶσαν τὴν πίστιν ὥστε ὄρη μεθιστάναι, ἀγάπην δὲ μὴ ἔχω, οὐθέν εἰμι.
kjv: And though I have the gift of prophecy, and understand all mysteries, and all knowledge; and though I have all faith, so that I could remove mountains, and have not charity, I am nothing.
modern: And if I have prophecy and know all the mysteries and all knowledge, and if I have all faith so as to move mountains but do not have love, I am nothing.

greek: νυνὶ δὲ μένει πίστις, ἐλπίς, ἀγάπη· τὰ τρία ταῦτα, μείζων δὲ τούτων ἡ ἀγάπη.
kjv: And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity.
modern: And now faith, hope, and love remain, these three; but the greatest of these is love.

greek: ὅτε ἤμην νήπιος, ἐλάλουν ὡς νήπιος, ἐφρόνουν ὡς νήπιος, ἐλογιζόμην ὡς νήπιος· ὅτε γέγονα ἀνήρ, κατήργηκα τὰ τοῦ νηπίου.
kjv: When I was a child, I spake as a child, I understood as a child, I thought as a child: but when I became a man, I put away childish things.
modern: When I was a child, I spoke like a child, I thought like a child, I reasoned like a child; when I have become an adult, I have put an end to the things of the child.

greek: πάντα στέγει, πάντα πιστεύει, πάντα ἐλπίζει, πάντα ὑπομένει.
kjv: Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things.
modern: It bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.

greek: βλέπομεν γὰρ ἄρτι δι’ ἐσόπτρου ἐν αἰνίγματι, τότε δὲ πρόσωπον πρὸς πρόσωπον· ἄρτι γινώσκω ἐκ μέρους, τότε δὲ ἐπιγνώσομαι καθὼς καὶ ἐπεγνώσθην.
kjv: For now we see through a glass, darkly; but then face to face: now I know in part; but then shall I know even as also I am known.
modern: For now we see through a mirror, in an enigma, but then face to face; now I know in part, but then I will know fully, just as I also was fully known.

greek: ἐκ μέρους γὰρ γινώσκομεν καὶ ἐκ μέρους προφητεύομεν·
kjv: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.
modern: For we know in part, and we prophesy in part.
